stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In hydrology, the volume of a lake can be described in units called cubic kilometers.
  • One cubic kilometer is equal to one billion cubic meters of water.
  • The volume of Lake Tahoe is 156 cubic kilometers.
  • The volume of Lake Superior is 12,100 cubic kilometers.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the volume of Lake Superior. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: With a volume of 12,100 cubic kilometers, Lake Superior is far larger than Lake Tahoe.
In hydrology, the volume of a lake can be described in units called cubic kilometers, and one cubic kilometer equals one billion cubic meters.
The volume of lakes, like Lake Tahoe, can be described in units called cubic kilometers.
Lake Tahoe is smaller than Lake Superior, which is one of the Great Lakes.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the volume of Lake Superior, noting that it has a volume of 12,100 cubic kilometers and that it is far larger than Lake Tahoe.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence introduces cubic kilometers as a unit of measurement; it doesn’t emphasize the volume of Lake Superior. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence makes a generalization about how the volume of lakes can be measured; it doesn’t emphasize the volume of Lake Superior. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence mentions Lake Superior but doesn’t emphasize its specific volume.
